user_query:
Please set up our local sales lead CRM and run an end-to-end import, scoring, follow-up scheduling, conversion marking, reporting, and docs validation. Read all inputs from input/. Then write all artifacts to output/ as specified below.

Steps:
1) Import new prospects from input/leads.csv (columns: name,email,company,source). Add each lead via the local CLI. Deduplicate by email: if a duplicate is detected on add, skip it and log the skip.
2) Apply scoring based on input/scoring_rules.json. Treat rules as additive but cap total at 100. Record a clear reason string per scoring update (e.g., "Source:web +20; Domain:bigco.com +40"). Use the CLI to update scores. If a rule doesn’t apply, don’t add points.
3) Schedule follow-ups from input/followups.yaml. Match entries by email, then create follow-up reminders with the provided YYYY-MM-DD date and note. Only schedule valid dates; if an entry has an invalid date format, skip it and log the skip.
4) If a follow-up entry includes convert: true, mark that lead as converted using the CLI. Use deal_value when provided.
5) Generate a pipeline report for the current month using the CLI and save the exact CLI stdout to output/pipeline.txt.
6) Create documentation in output/docs/:
   - output/docs/README.md: a concise overview of what you imported, how you scored, scheduled, and converted leads, plus a links section.
   - output/docs/pipeline_report.md: a summary of the pipeline metrics pulled from output/pipeline.txt, including a link to ../pipeline.txt.
   Ensure README.md includes a working relative link to ./pipeline_report.md and pipeline_report.md includes a working relative link to ../pipeline.txt.
7) Run a markdown link validation over output/docs/ and save a JSON report to output/lint_report.json. The report must include totalFiles, brokenLinksCount, and details[]. We expect brokenLinksCount to be 0.
8) Write an execution log of every CLI command you ran (with arguments) to output/actions.md.
9) Write an import summary to output/import_log.json with: total_rows, added, skipped_duplicates, errors (number), and details[] with one object per row containing email and status ("added"|"duplicate"|"error").

Use only relative workspace paths. Read inputs from input/. Write outputs exactly at the paths listed under output/ above.
